chefGuide.title: Administrative Guide
chefGuide.subtitle: Procedures and regulations for academic internship management.
chefGuide.aboutContent: This guide presents the official academic procedures for internship management by Department Heads. It covers contract validation, intern supervision, and internship closure.
chefGuide.conventionIntro: The internship contract is the legal cornerstone of the internship. Its validation certifies the pedagogical consistency of the project.
chefGuide.conventionStep1Text: Automatic alerts when a student signs a project approved by a company.
chefGuide.conventionStep2Text: Validation must occur within 3 business days.
chefGuide.conventionStep3Text: Verify that the missions match the educational objectives before signing.
chefGuide.absencesIntro: Monitor incidents reported by companies. Unjustified absences can impact the final internship validation.
chefGuide.reportsIntro: Each intern must submit their reports on the platform. Your role is to evaluate them to award ECTS credits.
chefGuide.closureIntro: The internship is closed by the company's final evaluation. You validate skill acquisition based on professional feedback and the academic report.
chefGuide.supportItem2: Data compliant with current academic standards.

workflow.label: Offer & Application
workflow.desc: The company posts an offer. The student applies with their CV.

workflow.label: Selection
workflow.desc: The company reviews CVs and accepts or refuses the application.

workflow.label: Agreement
workflow.desc: The Department validates and generates the signed internship agreement.

workflow.label: Internship
workflow.desc: The internship begins. Absences are tracked in real time.

workflow.label: Report
workflow.desc: The student submits their final internship report in PDF format.

workflow.label: Evaluation
workflow.desc: The tutor submits the final evaluation. Scores are archived.

faq.q: How do I apply for an internship?
faq.a: Browse available offers in the "Search Offers" section, open the offer of your choice, and click "Apply". Your CV must be uploaded to your profile before you can apply.

faq.q: Where can I track the status of my applications?
faq.a: Go to "Application Tracking" in the sidebar. Each application shows its current status: Pending, Accepted, or Refused.

faq.q: What happens after my application is accepted?
faq.a: The company accepts your application, then the Department validates and uploads a signed agreement. Once the agreement is validated, your internship is officially active.

faq.q: How do I submit my internship report?
faq.a: Navigate to "My Internship" and use the report submission section to upload your PDF report. The deadline is set by your Department.

faq.q: How do I reset my password?
faq.a: Click "Forgot password?" on the login page. A reset code will be sent to your registered email address.
Your company supervisor reports absences directly on the platform. You can justify absences by uploading a document in the "Absences" section.

faq.q: Where can I view my internship evaluation?
faq.a: Once your internship is complete and your supervisor has submitted their evaluation, it will appear under "My Evaluations" with scores per criterion and comments.

terms.title: Account Responsibility
terms.body: You are responsible for keeping your login credentials confidential. Any activity performed under your account is your responsibility.

terms.title: Acceptable Use
terms.body: The platform is reserved exclusively for academic internship management. Any fraudulent use may result in account suspension.

terms.title: Data & Privacy
terms.body: Your personal data is stored securely and used only for coordination between you, companies and the Department.

terms.title: Document Authenticity
terms.body: All uploaded documents (CV, reports, justifications) must be authentic. Any falsification constitutes a serious academic misconduct.

about.desc: internHub is the platform that brings students, companies, and schools together for successful internships.

2.desc: Validate agreements, monitor ongoing internships, and communicate easily with all parties involved.
